title: Document and harden Meshtastic adapter queue_free contract
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.2 prevents partial full-sync enqueue bursts when the adapter ops provide queue_free; the production gateway wires that hook, but custom/non-gateway adapter users without queue_free retain best-effort enqueue semantics. What needs to be done: document the queue_free preflight contract in the adapter API and evaluate whether non-gateway callers should get a required hook, rollback behavior, or explicit degraded-mode tests. Acceptance criteria: adapter.h/docs state the guarantee and limitation clearly; tests cover the chosen behavior for callers with and without queue_free.
notes:
Implementation evidence for Meshtastic adapter queue_free contract:
- Documented lichen_meshtastic_adapter_ops.queue_free in adapter.h as an optional preflight hook for WantConfig sync bursts, not a reservation/rollback mechanism.
- Documented Meshtastic queue preflight semantics in docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md: accurate same-queue queue_free can prevent partial sync bursts when capacity is insufficient; stale/wrong queue_free or later enqueue failures can still leave partial output; no-hook callers intentionally get best-effort partial enqueue behavior.
- Added native_sim ztests for queue_free preflight on stage 69420, no-hook degraded backpressure on stage 69421, stale queue_free partial full-sync behavior, and no-hook full-sync partial backpressure. Existing tests already covered stage 69421 and full-sync accurate queue_free preflight.
